subject: Case dismissed
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
body:
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Today, the prosecutor dropped the false charges against me.

This was a great victory and a long time coming. I've said from the beginning that I am innocent.

This experience has also been humbling, and I've emerged from it a changed man.

I believe that in all of our lives, we have to deal with pain, and that if we deal with it in the right way, we can learn wisdom.

We all have to deal with suffering, but if we deal with it in the right way, we can emerge with strength.

I also believe, as many people of faith do, that even in the hardest situations, we can find blessings.

Above all, I am sorry for the pain that this process and my actions have caused my family, my friends, and the people of Missouri.

I am extraordinarily grateful for the tremendous patience and courage of friends, family, and people of faith, who have all recognized that in time comes the truth.

We have a great mission before us. And at this time, I'd ask people of goodwill to come together so that we may continue to do good together.

Thank you,
Eric

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

| **"Governor Makes It Official: Will Veto Budget, Taxes****"**
Associated Press
By David Jordan
May 14, 2018 Republican Gov. Phil Scott on Monday said he was left with no choice but to veto the budget and property tax bills after the measures passed by lawmakers over the weekend violated his promise to avoid any new taxes. During a speech at the Vermont Fuel Conference in Killington, Scott said he planned to hold the line on any new taxes and fees for the second year in a row. "This approach is unacceptable to me. It doesn't make any sense to me. And, based on what I have heard over the last two years — and really, for most of my political career — it's unacceptable to most taxpayers like you," Scott said in his prepared remarks. Scott added he is optimistic that he will be able to strike a deal with the Legislature before the end of the fiscal year on June 30. The governor has not issued vetoes yet. If the Legislature does reconvene it would likely be in June. "I believe, we can still work together on my plan — or an alternative path — because we're really not that far apart," Scott said. The budget passed both the House and Senate with overwhelming support from the three major parties, and Democratic leaders have said they are proud of the budget. The governor and Legislature disagree over how to spend extra funds, a combination of increased tax revenue and money from a settlement with the tobacco industry. Scott would like to place the $58 million into an education fund to avert property tax increases, but legislators say the funds can be put to better use by buying down future pension obligations. Democrats accuse the governor of playing politics by attempting to avoid a tax increase in an election year. "And those things, in their entirety, to the magnitude he's talking about, are just a non-starter with us," Democratic House Speaker Mitzi Johnson told Vermont Public Radio. Scott also hinted at a larger battle over public education in the state. During the speech, Scott said as the state continues to lose both population and students, it is necessary to examine how the public education system is both organized and funded. "By not addressing the inefficiencies in the system, it has become unaffordable, and we face yet another property tax hike this year," Scott said.

subject: Don't forget to vote absentee today!

body:
| [](http://coreystewartusa.nationbuilder.com/?e=ed823c4651e3363eb3aaad58e45e9481&utm_source=coreystewartusa&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=voteabsentee&n=1)
---
Peter -- If you think there's any chance you will not be able to make it to the polls on June 12th, apply to vote absentee online or by mail now!

Absentee voting has been underway for over a week and Corey needs your help defeating the establishment for the June 12th Primary.

In order to apply to vote absentee, you must provide a reason. There are many acceptable reasons, but most include one of the following:
* You are working and commuting to/from home for 11 or more hours between 6:00 AM and 7:00 PM on Election Day
* You are a student attending college outside of where you live
* You will be out of town on Election Day
* For a full list of reasons for voting absentee, [**please visit the Virginia Department of Elections site here.**](https://coreystewartusa.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.elections.virginia.gov%2Fcasting-a-ballot%2Fabsentee-voting%2F&e=ed823c4651e3363eb3aaad58e45e9481&utm_source=coreystewartusa&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=voteabsentee&n=2)

To apply online for an absentee ballot, [**please follow this link.**](https://coreystewartusa.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fvote.elections.virginia.gov%2FVoterInformation&e=ed823c4651e3363eb3aaad58e45e9481&utm_source=coreystewartusa&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=voteabsentee&n=3)

You can also apply for an absentee ballot by mail, fax, or email. To print out your absentee ballot, [**please follow this link.**](https://coreystewartusa.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.elections.virginia.gov%2FFiles%2FForms%2FVoterForms%2FVirginiaAbsenteeBallotApplication.pdf&e=ed823c4651e3363eb3aaad58e45e9481&utm_source=coreystewartusa&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=voteabsentee&n=4)

Once you apply, you should receive your absentee ballot in the mail within a few weeks.

Lastly, you can also complete an absentee ballot application in person by visiting your local registrar – but this must be done up to three days before the primary on June 12th. To find your local voter registration office, [**please follow this link.**](https://coreystewartusa.nationbuilder.com/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fvote.elections.virginia.gov%2FVoterInformation%2FPublicContactLookup&e=ed823c4651e3363eb3aaad58e45e9481&utm_source=coreystewartusa&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=voteabsentee&n=5)

Here is a list of important deadlines ahead of the Republican Primary on June 12th:
* Deadline to register to vote, or update an existing registration:**Monday, May 21, 2018**
* Deadline to request an absentee ballot to be mailed: **Tuesday, June 5, 2018, by 5:00 pm**
* Deadline to request an absentee ballot by appearing in-person:**Saturday, June 9, 2018, by 5:00 pm**

If you qualify to vote absentee, we hope Corey can count on your support in the fight to replace Tim Kaine with an America First Conservative!

********This fight is going to be tough, but if you stand with Corey today, you'll help elect the best candidate to take on Tim Kaine in November!

Thanks,

Team Corey

name: The White House
subject: Honoring officers killed in the line of duty
body:
Your 1600 Daily:
| | [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4aa8de8e59b289f394f49047e37c2c7a7a1cbc7f813ad885ef5743ac22ad9bfb1656e59f2f5c00843be4fb2d13d5d7c8a1a0d99d08982e42) The White House • May 15, 2018
## The Day Ahead
President Donald J. Trump and Vice President Mike Pence are paying tribute to law enforcement officers who lost their lives in the line of duty in 2017. [Watch live at 11 a.m. ET.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4aa8de8e59b289f3d8d47cc4e6d4ad74dd478eb82f5e2197335cb539e67e04e5eed505fbcdb918706f4b62c6cbeb13eb06aeffe8001bb9d4) |
---
|

## Honoring officers killed in the line of duty
**Last year, 129 law enforcement officers died in the line of duty.** This morning, at the 37th Annual National Peace Officers’ Memorial Service, President Trump will join senior officials from across the Administration to honor these heroes’ sacrifices and recognize their families.
**In May of 1982, 125 people gathered for the First National Peace Officers’ Memorial Service.** Over the last 37 years, the Fraternal Order of Police has honored more than 5,000 law enforcement officers nationwide.
**Today, more than 20,000 officers, families, and friends** of law enforcement attend the annual Memorial Service. In addition to the 129 officers who died in 2017, this year’s event will honor 70 additional officers whose deaths were previously unrecognized. Many of these 70 officers died from illnesses related to service during the September 11 attacks.
[Watch President Trump’s address live at 11 a.m. ET.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4aa8de8e59b289f3d8d47cc4e6d4ad74dd478eb82f5e2197335cb539e67e04e5eed505fbcdb918706f4b62c6cbeb13eb06aeffe8001bb9d4)
**More** : [How the Trump Administration stood up for law and order in 2017 ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4aa8de8e59b289f3cae2a0b5b48a4c124dc951d7c872cbd2263c12778c31d1c4567a3bf6f8b155d20165f41ca9bd4f2cb6079bc4dd092127)

---
|

## 70 years of Israel’s independence
**Yesterday, the world celebrated the 70th anniversary of Israel’s independence**. To mark the occasion, President Trump fulfilled a signature campaign promise by officially opening the new United States Embassy in Jerusalem. He has also formally recognized Jerusalem as Israel’s capital.
**Vice President Pence spoke last night at the Israeli Embassy in Washington**. “I bring greetings and congratulations from a leader who has done more to bring our two countries closer together in a year than any President in the past 70 years,” the Vice President said of President Trump.
**“We gather here to celebrate nothing less than a miracle of history** : the day when the Jewish people ended the longest exile of any people,” Vice President Pence said. “The United States of America was proud to be the first nation in the world to recognize the State of Israel seven decades ago.”
[Watch highlights of Vice President Pence’s speech.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4aa8de8e59b289f305c3964aa5f6e76a75c32d1cbbf68dde8248e67d037b77fd7e5dbad5a3736ba5507a760700788e65c4dc7c590642bbac)
**What they’re saying** : [Support for the new U.S. Embassy in Jerusalem ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4aa8de8e59b289f391bc1afbf6cc2b2d995f3f9e9a11beb739b7a138c2f54a8b8164108e22f55cfd8c73b475a2f438b75424deb8ce008f68)

No ifs, ands, or buts about it -- to have any chance at taking back the Senate this year, we have to win in Wisconsin, Derek.

Trump won here in 2016, and Scott Walker's party holds all but one of Wisconsin's major statewide elected offices: Mine. I'm facing a tough re-election this year -- and I need your help to hold on to my seat and give Democrats a chance to flip the Senate.

**Split a $10 donation between my campaign and the DNC to support my re-election and elect Democrats in all 50 states this year.**

** This election is about choices, Derek -- and nowhere are those choices more clear than in Wisconsin. We get to decide between our vision of health care as a right for all Americans or Paul Ryan's plan to let families go bankrupt in medical emergencies. We get to decide if women control our own medical decisions. We get to decide if lesbian, gay, and transgender people enjoy the same rights as our straight friends. And we get to decide if we want to build the middle class or just let the rich get richer.

This is our democracy, and in a democracy we decide what's possible. The choice is yours: **Should we put in the work to elect Democrats who will fight for our values, or let Republicans like Paul Ryan and Scott Walker keep winning?**

name: Donna Edwards
subject: Prince George's County Moms Deserve More
body:
|
---
[ ](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff15/767134fb/5c845923/43383a81/844398732/VEsH/ "http://www.donnaforprincegeorges.org/")

| | Hello Friend, I spent a glorious Mother's Day with my son, Jared and my mother. The day was a time to reflect on both the lessons my mother taught me about living life in service to others and to reflect on my life as a Mom working to provide for Jared when he was a child. In Prince George's County, some mothers make sacrifices that they should not have to make. No mother should have to settle for making only $11.50 an hour to feed, clothe, and provide a home for their children. I have a vision for Prince George's County that includes raising the minimum wage in the County to $15 an hour so that working moms and their families do not have to survive on "just enough", but enough to meet their responsibilities. After rent, that still only leaves $800 for everything else--child care, groceries, utilities, insurance. [**I want to make this vision of a more inclusive Prince George 's County Executive a reality. In order to do that, I need your help to win the June 26th Primary. Can you make a donation of $10 or more to my campaign today?**](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff15/767134fb/5c845923/43383a9e/844398732/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-website?refcode=website") Life experience and my career have taught me that the mothers of Prince George's County deserve more. Pew Research studies tell us that the average salary for women who do not work in nonprofits or government is 23% lower than men, while almost 3 times more women are heads of household. As Prince George's County Executive, addressing equal pay will be paramount to my goal of lessening the impact of the pay gap on working mothers.**[I believe that when women--especially mothers-- are paid the same salary as men, everyone thrives. Make a donation $10 or more today to invest in my vision for working moms.](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff15/767134fb/5c845923/43383a9e/844398732/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-website?refcode=website")** Thank you very much for your support, and for all you do. Together, we will build a stronger Prince George's County for working mothers and their children. Donna

body:
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/isxzg4Ulv-feO2eNGltRc_qw5HlW5AKUZyGwrWQQglk=/EjocyeoiqMMdnG-WXr-aAH9-ct0ruPWPbOvVO7n-0iQ=) Friends and Neighbors -- One of the major reasons I decided to run for Chief Financial Officer is to serve as an important check and balance on the Governor's mansion, so that your best interests - and the proper allocation of your hard earned tax dollars - are the priority, not the Governor's buddies. Unfortunately, Governor Scott -- and the current CFO for that matter -- doesn't seem to share that same outlook. In fact, in a surprising turn of events, It looks like Rick Scott has decided to drain the swamp right into the Department of Revenue. Per a recent article in the Tampa Bay Times, "The Florida Department of Revenue has ousted top employees and kept positions vacant for months to make room for many of Governor Rick Scott's loyal staffers who will be out of their jobs when he leaves office this year, even though none of them have any experience in that department's main responsibility: tax administration. [](https://act.myngp.com/el/isxzg4Ulv-feO2eNGltRc_qw5HlW5AKUZyGwrWQQglk=/DgOGY1P7gt95ROTMU9GmlTSKsUtmmE_1-eYsdKqu2SY=?am=3&recipientId=EsCT1N2SCLZJkIJRRTPlB2&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&) [Help Jeremy Fight Cabinet Cronyism. disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
When it comes to backwards legislation, governors have the final say. | [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd796a8/2f5ea88d/71b266e0/2cbcd1bc/4141405337/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mid-may-deadline?utm_source=ema_2018-05-15_mid-month-1&refcode=ema_2018-05-15_mid-month-1&utm_campaign=mid-month-1&utm_medium=ema") Friend, **It matters who is governor!** This month when Iowa's legislature passed the strictest abortion bill in the country, effectively outlawing abortions six weeks after conception, Gov. Kim Reynolds could have vetoed it. Instead, she championed that bill, leading one of the most serious attack on women's most basic rights and freedoms our nation has ever seen. When lawmakers in Kansas and Oklahoma passed a bill allowing adoption agencies to prevent LGBT families from taking in children, the governors could have stopped this outrageous discrimination. Instead, they used the power of this office to sign that discrimination into law. It's beyond offensive -- it's just plain backwards. **With Republican governors signing backwards laws across America and Donald Trump threatening so much of what we value from the White House, it has never been more clear why it matters who servers as governor.** [**I need you to stand with me, to show your support and fight for what we believe in. I need to know you're by my side. We're coming up to our mid-May deadline -- can you chip in to support all that we're fighting for here in Spokane?**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd796a8/2f5ea88d/71b266e0/2cbcd1bc/4141405337/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mid-may-deadline?utm_source=ema_2018-05-15_mid-month-1&refcode=ema_2018-05-15_mid-month-1&utm_campaign=mid-month-1&utm_medium=ema") _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [EXPRESS DONATE $10 »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd796a8/2f5ea88d/71b266e0/2cbcd1b3/4141405337/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mid-may-deadline?utm_source=ema_2018-05-15_mid-month-1&refcode=ema_2018-05-15_mid-month-1&utm_campaign=mid-month-1&utm_medium=ema&express_lane=true&amount=10")

Leading their states away from backwards legislation is one of the greatest responsibilities governors wield. Governors set the tone for the policies their state will pass -- which should be focused on the big issues facing our state and our nation, instead of unconstitutional political plays that limit a woman's right to her own reproductive freedom, or strip LGBTQ families of their opportunities.
A governor's role is even more important in 2020 as we head towards redistricting. name: The Sanders Institute
subject: 40 million fewer votes than 2016
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
We call ourselves a Democracy--a government of the people, by the people, and for the people.
#
Friend,
We call ourselves a Democracy--a government of the people, by the people, and for the people. However, the sad truth is that America has very low rates of participation in our "democracy." In 2016 fewer than six-in-ten Americans voted, and in this year's off-year election, 40 million fewer Americans are predicted to vote.
For the past six weeks, we at the Sanders Institute with one of our founding fellows, Senator Nina Turner, have highlighted some of the glaring issues in the American voting system.
We want to continue to educate about these important issues. Help us produce more papers and videos highlighting these and other important issues in the United States of America.
**[DONATE»](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ab58/bed43eb/6e64a371/75fdbe4b/3450654125/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sandersinstitute?refcode=em180515")**
Millions of Americans around the country are restricted from voting and millions more face hurdles that seem insurmountable.
These past weeks we have covered:
**Tuesday and Early Voting:** The inconveniences of Tuesday voting and how early voting, no-excuse absentee voting and voting on the weekend or holidays could help Americans vote.
**Unaffiliated Voters:** The disenfranchisement of many of the 46% of Americans who identify as independents because they cannot participate in closed primaries.
**Voter ID Laws:** The 21 million Americans who do not have government-issued ID and how states with strict voter ID rules bar those Americans from voting.
**Felony Disenfranchisement:** The 6.1 million Americans who cannot vote because of felony disenfranchisement.
**Voter Registration:** The complicated and varying registration rules that require Americans to register up to 31 days before election day.
These are some of the glaring issues in our voting system, but they are not the only ones...
**American Territories:** 4.4 million Americans in American territories and the District are represented by only one "delegate" each in the House of Representatives (who cannot vote on the House floor), and no American citizens living in the territories may vote for president.
**The Electoral College:** Due to the electoral college, some votes count more than others. Votes in smaller rural states tend to count more toward the final result than those in more densely populated areas.
[**Click here to read the full paper.**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ab58/bed43eb/6e64a371/75fdbe4a/3450654125/VEsE/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/why-dont-americans-vote")
Thanks for staying engaged,
The Sanders Institute Team

In Congress, our representatives are doing the same thing. From countless attempts to repeal the Affordable Care Act, to attempts to change Title X, the federal family planning law, disregard for women's healthcare has never been higher.

**[It's National Women's Health Week, so join Paul Davis and stand up to protect women's healthcare here in Kansas and across the country. name: Ben Jealous
subject: Do it now
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
There's too much at stake to step back.

Friend,
Former Maryland Governor William Donald Schaefer used to have a motto: "Do it now."
I've been thinking about that a lot lately, as I hear our representatives hem and haw about how to make health care more affordable, or how to help end the crushing student debt crisis. There's a lot of talk, and not much action. I remember his words: **"Do it now."**
That's the campaign we're running. Sure we have big plans, but we have strategies to get those plans done now. Because folks can't keep waiting for this change to come -- it starts with us, right here, right now.
That's why I'm asking you to step up and invest in this campaign. We have an aggressive mid-month fundraising deadline at midnight, and it's one of our last before next month's primary. **[To get our plans done we need the funds to fight -- will you donate $5 or more now?](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a61b/7ab0e2c4/7fb55ef5/349a4726/38658627/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-mdmn-may?refcode=OHQ05158A")**
_Or, if your payment information is saved with ActBlue, click below and your contribution will go through immediately:_
| [**$5 »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a61b/7ab0e2c4/7fb55ef5/349a4721/38658627/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-mdmn-may?amount=5&refcode=OHQ05158A&express_lane=true")

---
| | [**OTHER »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a61b/7ab0e2c4/7fb55ef5/349a472c/38658627/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-mdmn-may?refcode=OHQ05158A&express_lane=true")
---
It's frustrating to me to see Maryland stand still -- I'm an organizer, and I'm used to working together, and working hard, to make progress.
I look at families struggling to pay their bills, and small businesses fighting to catch a break, and I know there's work to be done that can be done right now. So why are we standing still?
I'm running for governor because I know that together, we can make Maryland the progressive state we all want it to be -- one that works for everyone. If you believe it too, I'm asking you to join us. Because we have to do it now.
**[Tonight is one of our last fundraising deadlines before the June primary, so don't wait: Donate $5 or more today to fund the fight for a better Maryland.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a61b/7ab0e2c4/7fb55ef5/349a4726/38658627/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-mdmn-may?refcode=OHQ05158A")**
Thanks,
Ben

name: Zephyr Teachout
subject: Thank you, Peter, here's what's next
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Zephyr for NY
body:
We know that special interests and powerful people will do whatever they can to stop us.
#
Thank you, Peter. I'm so grateful to have your support as I consider running for Attorney General.
The race for New York Attorney General will be one of the most important races this year. From Albany to Washington to Wall Street, the amount of corruption in our system today is higher than ever, and our politics needs a root canal if we want to fix it and rebuild a system that works for the people.
We're living through a crisis in democracy. Our next AG has to be independent and unafraid of the establishment. She must be ready to investigate corruption in Albany, in real estate, and on Wall Street.
**Peter, you were already one of the first to step up and say you're ready for this campaign. Now I'm asking you to take the next step and become a Founding Donor:**
**[Please make a $3 contribution to my exploratory committee as I consider running for Attorney General.](https://zephyr.bsd.net/page/m/4d780fc6/6e822e2f/18ead842/41720692/3451055929/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA1MTUtMi1mdWxsIn0=/ "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-founding-donor?refcode=em180515-2-full")**
We know that special interests and powerful people will do whatever they can to stop us. They'll spend millions against us because they know Attorney General Teachout would be their worst nightmare.
They don't scare me because I have you.
The Attorney General has the potential to be an incredible force for good in our system, and it's not just about corruption (although that's extremely important). Some of my top priorities would be breaking up corporate monopolies, protecting our environment, and reforming our justice system.
With this election, we have the opportunity to restructure our priorities. Do we want someone who protects the boys club in Albany or Wall Street, or do we want someone who will fight for ordinary people and change how our system works?
**[Please, make a $3 donation now to become a Founding Donor. We need your support if we're going to take on corruption and lawlessness at the highest pinnacles of power.](https://zephyr.bsd.net/page/m/4d780fc6/6e822e2f/18ead842/41720692/3451055929/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA1MTUtMi1mdWxsIn0=/ "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-founding-donor?refcode=em180515-2-full")**
Thank you again for joining our team.
Best,
Zephyr

body:
Today is Election Day, Pennsylvania! If you haven't voted yet, **[confirm your polling place](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e67b6/51dbcc2a/1860ebd/3a24af8/2941427202/VEsH/ "https://www.pavoterservices.pa.gov/Pages/PollingPlaceInfo.aspx")** and go vote now.

CNBC: "President Donald Trump and a Republican candidate he backs hope to take a first step Tuesday toward flipping a Senate seat in swing state Pennsylvania."
Help Bob Casey keep PA's Senate seat blue:
[DONATE](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e67b6/51dbcc2a/1860ebd/3a24af9/2941427202/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiYm9iLWNhc2V5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbV8yMDE4MDUxNV9mcjFfUEFuZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiI1LDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180515_fr1_PAnd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")

Here's what you need to know about today's Republican Senate primary:
President Donald Trump has thrown his full support behind Congressman Lou Barletta, a former mayor who made his name on anti-immigrant ordinances so vile they were overturned in federal court. Trump even recorded a robocall for Barletta before the primary, making it clear that their friendship is going to be a major factor in the general election.

If Donald Trump's favored candidate wins tonight -- as most people expect -- national Republicans are going to pour more time and money into flipping this seat red. The president doesn't campaign for just anyone, but he's already appeared on stage with Barletta, tweeted his support and recorded that robocall.

**_[Today's primary is Trump's first step toward gaining another ally in the Senate, and Democrats need to be prepared to fight back hard. Donate $5 or whatever you can to Bob Casey before the polls close in PA.](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e67b6/51dbcc2a/1860ebd/3a24af9/2941427202/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiYm9iLWNhc2V5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbV8yMDE4MDUxNV9mcjFfUEFuZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiI1LDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180515_fr1_PAnd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

Trump, Barletta and their Republican friends are going to keep attacking Bob, both because he has been a firewall against their extreme agenda and because they see an opportunity to gain a Senate seat in a state they won two years ago. One of Barletta's favorite talking points is that, if he had been in the Senate last year, the Affordable Care Act would have been repealed by now. The scary truth is that he's right, and that's why we can't take any chances this year.

**Bob needs to raise another $5,200 before the polls close at 8pm to start the general election on the right foot. Chip in now to help out:**

**_[secure.bobcasey.com/fightback](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e67b6/51dbcc2a/1860ebd/3a24af9/2941427202/VEsC/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiYm9iLWNhc2V5IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJlbV8yMDE4MDUxNV9mcjFfUEFuZCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiI1LDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180515_fr1_PAnd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

Thanks.

After Saturday’s resounding convention win, Wisconsin has never been closer to sending Tammy and her liberal policies packing.

Friend,

After Saturday’s resounding convention win, Wisconsin has never been closer to sending Tammy and her liberal policies packing.

But before that can happen, I need to make sure my name appears on the ballot in November. That’s where you come in.

[HELP GET LEAH ON THE BALLOT](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=10f9764b325c478194da4c3789309b37&_e=yquv_ySzGXJR-4VcrI8BkNwwh2nuydoBhgxeyr79LVF0y_YY7YehjX7ziQxY3Eot9RjIDujYyAvHGDUcIXLqkz-dCsLmzD88B9E_TuIO3CSKC1c5ThoZMpCF1htCTZijl7sGstTihqsGjIraSHs_rhWfUjzO7NApSYXqujfBIWwKGvXKzg2VjAZ2THQALiihaHK4ubIr4f0tBjwGhAhCMu0Bw4blNfiiYdtH5cvcL_Skk9B8R2O4kD6_dI4gl4whTugr8k0GjeO134P5EarUHBDOiLp6VIIkM4t6N_arzsOuoulF1hGK6eeFSd_jolltNIEoz96m2aEe5Bs5lI1iFtBFeEK_5JpuoED7X0Vru_GFCqivpmf4cEFOf8F0Mox3Woa--vHB8dBBzqOZR4ZkUlyQVMHbNoH6Riax7zAJksWuCHR6IY5FR-B-35tHJDx6)

We have thousands of signatures to gather, and we’re running short on time.

Leah

That moment got me started on my mission to expand opportunities for education across Wisconsin, but we have a whole lot further to go. What are the [issues](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=10f9764b325c478194da4c3789309b37&_e=yquv_ySzGXJR-4VcrI8BkGkRrRMiU9aaueVZiuvErBwPT3l20Elc3ERjOLnJNUpE-I9_pKGwIcirFkLzKazUIqjeV1gSib3xh3GEQIJqdUVG6oyG2uAvg0B7_y5-D5MSN0wfIaT1FeI_MvIbx5sagidvUih2J_BNgJFykVErE7b5N0suOqlKSGO0YeLWR6LVnM3UFWhEvmJLlTBE8phz8nMIQnFr-aYG8TqxOfJzJ8DsdUlqVPvqT99MLnfxuZGAZgmmiEFX7Ba7qACLSOZmmze01h-BVimsvI0YEuMulCF26s_MN6M7SiZrRliu-l25NbwH1kBS19525sJljkMnFE0fTNfQizkEnsMXAwu8x51iGZlK5w4Uzto3CbwVcn9-) that you care about? #
Don't miss this.

Friend,
We wanted to make sure you saw Ben's note -- because this is the last call before our midnight deadline.
He said it best: We can't keep waiting. We have to do it now.
**[If you're ready to make real progress here in Maryland, donate $5 or more before midnight.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a61f/7ab0e2c4/7fb7f868/349a44a6/1426701447/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-mdmn-may?refcode=OHQ05158B")**

---
Thanks,
Team Jealous

\------------ Forwarded Message ------------
From: **Ben Jealous**
Date: Tuesday, May 15
Subject: Do it now
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
#
Friend,
Former Maryland Governor William Donald Schaefer used to have a motto: "Do it now."
I've been thinking about that a lot lately, as I hear our representatives hem and haw about how to make health care more affordable, or how to help end the crushing student debt crisis. There's a lot of talk, and not much action. I remember his words: **"Do it now."**
That's the campaign we're running. Sure we have big plans, but we have strategies to get those plans done now. Because folks can't keep waiting for this change to come -- it starts with us, right here, right now.
That's why I'm asking you to step up and invest in this campaign. We have an aggressive mid-month fundraising deadline at midnight, and it's one of our last before next month's primary. **[To get our plans done we need the funds to fight -- will you donate $5 or more now?](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a61f/7ab0e2c4/7fb7f868/349a44a6/1426701447/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-mdmn-may?refcode=OHQ05158B")**

---
| | [**$100 »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a61f/7ab0e2c4/7fb7f868/349a44a2/1426701447/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-mdmn-may?amount=100&refcode=OHQ05158B&express_lane=true")
---
| [**$250 »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a61f/7ab0e2c4/7fb7f868/349a44ad/1426701447/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-mdmn-may?amount=250&refcode=OHQ05158B&express_lane=true")
---
| | [**OTHER »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a61f/7ab0e2c4/7fb7f868/349a44ac/1426701447/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-mdmn-may?refcode=OHQ05158B&express_lane=true")
---
It's frustrating to me to see Maryland stand still -- I'm an organizer, and I'm used to working together, and working hard, to make progress.
I look at families struggling to pay their bills, and small businesses fighting to catch a break, and I know there's work to be done that can be done right now. So why are we standing still?
I'm running for governor because I know that together, we can make Maryland the progressive state we all want it to be -- one that works for everyone. If you believe it too, I'm asking you to join us. Because we have to do it now.
**[Tonight is one of our last fundraising deadlines before the June primary, so don't wait: Donate $5 or more today to fund the fight for a better Maryland.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a61f/7ab0e2c4/7fb7f868/349a44a6/1426701447/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-mdmn-may?refcode=OHQ05158B")**
Thanks,
Ben

name: Rahsheim Wright
subject: Prince George's County Moms Deserve More
body:
|
---
[ ](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff13/767134fb/5f7d0595/43383945/2325307138/VEsH/ "http://www.donnaforprincegeorges.org/")

| | Hello Friend, I wanted to make sure you saw Donna's note below. Donna is a leader who understands what working moms in Prince George's County need in order to provide for themselves and their families. **[Donna is running a grassroots campaign for Prince George's County Executive, and we are counting on your support. Will you make a contribution of $10 or more today?](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff13/767134fb/5f7d0595/43383942/2325307138/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-website?refcode=website")** Rahsheim Wright Campaign Manager **\-------Forwarded Message-------** Hello Friend, I spent a glorious Mother's Day with my son, Jared and my mother. The day was a time to reflect on both the lessons my mother taught me about living life in service to others and to reflect on my life as a Mom working to provide for Jared when he was a child. In Prince George's County, some mothers make sacrifices that they should not have to make. No mother should have to settle for making only $11.50 an hour to feed, clothe, and provide a home for their children. I have a vision for Prince George's County that includes raising the minimum wage in the County to $15 an hour so that working moms and their families do not have to survive on "just enough", but enough to meet their responsibilities. After rent, that still only leaves $800 for everything else--child care, groceries, utilities, insurance. [**I want to make this vision of a more inclusive Prince George 's County Executive a reality. In order to do that, I need your help to win the June 26th Primary. Can you make a donation of $10 or more to my campaign today?**](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff13/767134fb/5f7d0595/43383942/2325307138/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-website?refcode=website") Life experience and my career have taught me that the mothers of Prince George's County deserve more. Pew Research studies tell us that the average salary for women who do not work in nonprofits or government is 23% lower than men, while almost 3 times more women are heads of household. As Prince George's County Executive, addressing equal pay will be paramount to my goal of lessening the impact of the pay gap on working mothers.**[I believe that when women--especially mothers-- are paid the same salary as men, everyone thrives. Make a donation $10 or more today to invest in my vision for working moms.](http://action.donnaforprincegeorges.org/page/m/74feff13/767134fb/5f7d0595/43383942/2325307138/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dfe-website?refcode=website")** Thank you very much for your support, and for all you do. Together, we will build a stronger Prince George's County for working mothers and their children. Donna
